mnemonics: FOIL Your Rules: First-Order Inductive Learning helps you FOIL the problem by learning logical rules.
keyPoints: Sequential covering algorithms learn one rule at a time, remove covered examples, and repeat.
FOIL (First-Order Inductive Learner) is a top-down rule induction method for learning first-order Horn clauses.
Rule induction can be viewed as a search problem through the space of possible rules, guided by heuristics like information gain.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to remove covered examples after learning a rule, leading to redundant rules.
Confusing propositional rule learning (attributes) with first-order rule learning (relations and variables).
Neglecting the bias in the search (e.g., rule preference, search direction) which greatly impacts the learned theory.
